latest.title: FY26 Full Year Trading Update
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2472K
Made Tech Group PLC
30 June 2026
MADE TECH GROUP PLC
("Made Tech" or the "Group")
FY26 FULL YEAR TRADING UPDATE
69% growth in Adjusted EBITDA1 to £5.9m
FY26 expected to be ahead of recently upgraded market expectations
London, 30 June 2026
-
Made Tech Group Plc
, (AIM:MTEC) a leading provider of digital, data and technology services to the UK public sector, is pleased to announce a trading update for the year ended 31 May 2026 ("FY26").
FY26 highlights (unaudited)
·
Revenue of £58.9m ahead of market expectations
2
and up 27% on the prior year
·
Adjusted EBITDA
1
growth for FY26 of 69% to £5.9m
·
Adjusted EBITDA
1
margin increased by 250 bps year-on-year to c.10.0% - this margin improvement has been delivered ahead of previously indicated guidance
·
Net cash as at 31 May 2026 was £14.5 million (FY25: £10.4 million)
·
Made Tech remains debt-free with flexibility to pursue growth opportunities
Continued commercial momentum
·
Sales Bookings
3
momentum
including the recently awarded £19m contract with the Government Digital Service and a healthy Contracted Backlog
4
provides good revenue coverage for FY27 and beyond
·
Made Tech remains well positioned to benefit from the UK Governments' Spending Review announced in June 2025 and available from April 2026
·
AI is creating new product & growth opportunities
·
Made Tech will release its FY26 results in September and will provide an update on current trading at that time
Rory MacDonald, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"FY26 has been a transformational year for Made Tech. We have delivered strong revenue growth, materially improved profitability and cash generation, and have entered FY27 with significant positive momentum in our business. The UK public sector is entering a multi-decade AI transformation, creating a substantial long-term opportunity for trusted delivery partners like Made Tech. Recent contract awards reinforce our position at the centre of a number of critical programmes across government. With a strong balance sheet, high earnings visibility and an increasingly differentiated proposition, we believe Made Tech is well positioned to capitalise on these opportunities and we look forward to FY27 with confidence."
Notes:
All financials are based on unaudited figures.
1
Adjusted EBITDA has been adjusted for the exclusion of depreciation, amortisation, impairments, exceptional items and share-based payment charge.
2
Based on the latest published equity research, the Company understands current market consensus for the year ended 31 May 2026 (FY26) to be revenue of £57.5m, Adjusted EBITDA of £5.6m and cash of £13.5m, and for the year ended 31 May 2027 (FY27) to be revenue of £60.3m, Adjusted EBITDA of £6.0m and cash of £17.3m.
3
Sales Bookings represent the total value of sales contracts awarded in the Period, to be delivered in FY26-FY30. Net Sales Bookings includes the value of sales contracts previously recognised that lapsed during the Period.
4
Contracted Backlog is the value of contracted revenue that has not yet been recognised.
Enquiries:
Made Tech Group plc
Rory MacDonald, CEORichard Swinyard, CFO
investor-relations@madetech.com
Tel: +44 (0) 20 3397 7846
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nominated Adviser & Broker)
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Andrew Potts / Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7523 8000
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Made Tech awarded £19m contract with the GDS
latest.content:
15 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4775A
Made Tech Group PLC
15 April 2026
15 April 2026
MADE TECH GROUP PLC
("Made Tech" or the "Group")
Made Tech awarded £19 million contract with the Government Digital Service
Made Tech Group Plc, a leading provider of digital, data and technology services to the UK public sector, is pleased to announce that it has been awarded a new three year Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner contract with the Government Digital Service ("GDS"), part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology ("DSIT"), with a total contract value of £19 million. This three year contract award is expected to contribute to FY27 and beyond.
GDS sits at the centre of digital government, shaping common platforms, standards and ways of working that support the public, central government departments and the wider public sector. As a result, this contract further strengthens Made Tech's position at the heart of central government technology delivery and supports its wider strategic positioning across government.
Under the contract, Made Tech will act as the Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner to GDS, supporting the delivery of the next generation of technology used by GDS staff. The programme is intended to improve collaboration and productivity, strengthen security, and reduce overall cyber risk exposure.GDS has been a client of Made Tech since 2018, and this three-year contract award builds on that established relationship and the Group's track record of delivery in central government.
Following this contract award, the Group's year-to-date Sales Bookings have increased materially to £54 million, including approximately £41 million of new Sales Bookings secured so far in the second half of the current financial year across both existing and new clients and a range of contract sizes. The expected uptick in bookings during the second half supports current expectations for FY26 and enhances revenue visibility for FY27.
Rory MacDonald, CEO of Made Tech Group plc, commented:
"Being selected by GDS, a long-standing client, as its Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner is an important win for Made Tech. As the centre of digital government, GDS plays a critical role in shaping technology across government, and this award strengthens our position in one of the most important parts of the UK public sector technology market. We are particularly pleased to see the Sales Booking conversion of the pipeline we referenced at the Interim Results, further increasing our confidence in the outlook."
Enquiries:
Made Tech Group plc
Rory MacDonald, CEORichard Swinyard, CFO
Email: investor-relations@madetech.com

latest.title: Appointment of Chief Financial Officer
latest.content:
26 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4453U
Made Tech Group PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
MADE TECH GROUP PLC
("Made Tech" or the "Group")
Appointment of Chief Financial Officer
Made Tech Group Plc, a leading provider of digital, data and technology services to the UK public sector, is pleased to announce the appointment of Richard Swinyard as Chief Financial Officer with effect from 2 March 2026.
Richard is a highly experienced technology and services finance leader with a strong track record of supporting growth, operational improvement and value creation. Since 2012, he has operated in the US market, most recently as CFO of a private equity-backed IT and managed services business based in New York, where he led a period of significant organic growth, margin improvement and a sale of the business.
Prior to this, Richard was CFO and a board director at Agilisys, a UK-based provider of IT and digital transformation services to the public sector, where he played a central role in growing revenues and the order book, while also driving strong cash generation and operational improvements.
Earlier in his career, Richard held senior finance roles at listed companies, including Sanctuary Group Plc and Imprint Group Plc, after training at Deloitte. Richard is a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales and holds a BSc (Hons) in Management Science from Warwick Business School.
Rory MacDonald, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"I am pleased to welcome Richard to Made Tech. He brings deep experience in scaling technology and managed services businesses, with a strong track record of improving profitability, cash generation and operational discipline. His expertise will be important as we execute the next phase of our growth strategy, building on the progress achieved over recent years and strengthening the Group's financial performance."
Richard Swinyard commented:
"I am excited to join Made Tech at such an important point in its growth. The Group has a strong reputation in the UK public sector and a clear strategy focused on digital transformation and software-led growth. I look forward to working with Rory and the team to build on the progress achieved and deliver long-term value for shareholders."
Regulatory Disclosures
(in accordance with Rule 17 and Schedule Two paragraph (g) of the AIM Rules for Companies)

company_30s.what_they_do: Made Tech delivers cloud‑based software solutions and digital transformation services to public‑sector clients, helping them modernise legacy systems and adopt new technologies.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK government and large enterprises are steadily shifting to digital services. Made Tech sits in a niche that offers repeatable, high‑margin solutions, and a recent GDS contract puts it on a path to steady revenue growth.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just announced a £19 m GDS contract, posted solid interim results, and installed a new CFO. These moves suggest a pivot toward more stable, contract‑based income rather than chasing volatile product sales.

rns_narrative: Made Tech has secured a sizable government contract that will boost its revenue base and signal credibility with public‑sector buyers.
The interim earnings beat expectations, showing higher sales and a clean balance sheet, which reduces cash‑burn risk.
A new CFO and a series of leadership changes indicate a strategic shift toward disciplined financial management and a focus on long‑term growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are waiting for a clear sign of momentum before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the GDS deal and the strong earnings as a catalyst for a rebound, arguing the stock is undervalued after a prolonged sell‑off.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that sales still fell 65 % YoY and that the company’s survival depends on securing more contracts; any missed deadlines could halt the recovery.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat; the community is silent, which for contrarians signals that the next big move could catch everyone off‑guard.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning there is no existing hype to inflate the price – a perfect set‑up for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Official announcement of the GDS contract terms and any subsequent payment milestones.
The next earnings release – a surprise beat could trigger a spike.
Any new contract pipeline updates, especially from other government departments.
A clear sign of institutional buying, such as a large block trade or a new institutional investor filing.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least twice the 20‑day average coupled with a price breakout above the 50‑day moving average. A sustained close above the 200‑day line would confirm a reversal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a potential recovery. A single catalyst that confirms institutional interest will likely be enough to push it out of the crash zone.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price rebounds to even half of its former high, the upside is roughly 2‑3× current levels, matching the historical return of deep‑crash signals. The recent contract and earnings give a clear trigger for mean reversion.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: A missed contract deadline, a downgrade, or a sharp decline in sales could stall the bounce. The worst‑case is the stock staying at its current low for an extended period, which would limit upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low price, while upside could be 200–300 % if a full recovery occurs. The asymmetry favors the contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume spike that confirms a break above the 50‑day MA, size the position to 10 % of your portfolio, and set a stop 10 % below the entry level.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the price crosses the 200‑day MA with volume >2× average; then consider a small entry to test the bounce.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified – the only downside would be a prolonged flat market that keeps the price stuck.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Made Tech’s deep sell‑off, combined with a fresh government contract and solid earnings, creates a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play that hinges on a single volume‑confirmed catalyst.
